Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Ordered dithering with arbitrary or irregular colour palettes (2023) 29 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Intuitions for Tranformer Circuits 52 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. The way CTRL-C in Postgres CLI cancels queries is incredibly hack-y 47 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. How to attract AI bots to your open source project 123 upvotes, 18 comments
  5. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 188 upvotes, 42 comments
  6. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 385 upvotes, 102 comments
  7. Can you get root with only a cigarette lighter? (2024) 55 upvotes, 10 comments
  8. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 356 upvotes, 111 comments
  9. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 157 upvotes, 45 comments
  10. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 434 upvotes, 145 comments
  11. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 347 upvotes, 114 comments
  12. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 184 upvotes, 56 comments
  13. A Copy-Paste Bug That Broke PSpice AES-256 Encryption 39 upvotes, 10 comments
  14. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 576 upvotes, 265 comments
  15. Migrating the American express payment network, twice 66 upvotes, 22 comments
  16. LLMs predict my coffee 111 upvotes, 47 comments
  17. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 200 upvotes, 101 comments
  18. The future of version control 512 upvotes, 285 comments
  19. First and Lego Education Partnership Update 40 upvotes, 17 comments
  20. Why I love NixOS 299 upvotes, 210 comments
  21. They're Vibe-Coding Spam Now 82 upvotes, 49 comments
  22. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 386 upvotes, 286 comments
  23. Plane and ground vehicle collide at New York's LaGuardia airport halting flights 22 upvotes, 9 comments
  24. Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 117 upvotes, 85 comments
  25. You are not your job 144 upvotes, 161 comments
  26. Walmart: ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than website 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. What young workers are doing to AI-proof themselves 126 upvotes, 209 comments
  28. AI Proteomics Competition 2026 – $13K Prize, Internships and Compute Support 4 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. World Cup Trophy Theft: Gangsters, Spies and the Dog That Found It 4 upvotes, 2 comments
